text.title: TDD Worked Examples
text.summary: Python password validator walked through a full RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle; TypeScript email service; bug-fix reproduction pattern.

example.note: RED: Write failing test for minimum length.
example.code: def test_password_too_short_is_invalid(): validator = PasswordValidator() result = validator.validate("short") assert result.is_valid is False assert "at least 8 characters" in result.errors # Run: pytest — FAIL (PasswordValidator does not exist yet)

example.note: GREEN: Minimal code to pass the test.
REFACTOR: Extract rule list after all requirements are green.

rule.statement: For bug fixes: write a failing test that reproduces the bug before touching production code. The test docstring must reference the issue ID.

example.code: def test_discount_with_zero_quantity_does_not_raise(): """ BUG: ZeroDivisionError when cart contains zero-quantity item. Issue: #1234 """ calculator = DiscountCalculator() cart = Cart(items=[ CartItem(sku="A", quantity=0, price=Decimal("10.00")), CartItem(sku="B", quantity=2, price=Decimal("20.00")), ]) result = calculator.apply_percentage_discount(cart, Decimal("10")) assert result.total == Decimal("36.00")
